---
title: "Mauricio Rincón Bonilla"
---
Computational Materials Scientist – Chemical Engineer

Mauricio is a Senior Researcher and Ramón y Cajal Fellow at the Basque Center for Applied Mathematics (BCAM), co-leading the Energy Materials research line within the MSLMS area. His work bridges multiscale modelling and data-driven approaches to optimize nano- and mesostructured materials. With a focus on energy transport, storage, and catalysis, his specific research interests include next-generation batteries, high-entropy materials, hydrogen-alloy interactions, and diffusion under extreme confinement. Mauricio’s contributions to the field have been recognized with several prestigious awards, including the Ramón y Cajal Fellowship (2024), the Juan de la Cierva Fellowship (2019), an HPC Europa3 access grant (2018), and a Key Foreign Experts grant from Tongji University (2024).
